measures: The superpowers finding: whether a workflow-SUMMARIZING description pulls the model toward the description text instead of the skill body — mis-firing implement-feature on a single-milestone prompt. Variant A is implement-feature's real (workflow-paragraph) description; variant B trims it to a when-to-use-only disambiguation. Higher accuracy for B is the finding.

variantA.label: current — workflow-summary description
variantA.description: Orchestrate building a whole feature's milestones — connective tissue over implement-milestone + verify-milestone, in dependency order, enforcing the branch/PR/stack rules and stopping at the user's merge. Spawns fresh-context verifier subagents ([auto] parallel, [runtime] serial). Defaults to interleaved cadence but ALWAYS asks. Never merges.

cases.prompt: Build the whole notifications feature — all its milestones, verified end to end.
Build just the m2-harness milestone; its spec is already on main.
Implement the single milestone that this spec-change fans into.

variantB.label: trimmed — when-to-use only
variantB.description: Use to build a WHOLE feature's already-authored milestone specs end to end. NOT for a single milestone (that's implement-milestone).

cases.prompt: Build the whole notifications feature — all its milestones, verified end to end.
Build just the m2-harness milestone; its spec is already on main.
Implement the single milestone that this spec-change fans into.

note: The `detected` values are CANNED illustrative session outcomes carrying the measured superpowers finding, so judge.test.sh scores the A/B comparison deterministically offline. The live BASELINE run re-measures them against real `claude -p` sessions (the planned spend stop-point).
